# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Build an Elasticsearch snapshot configuration for automated backups. Configure snapshot repository to S3 or file share for the operations team. Implement snapshot creation and restoration functionality.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent datastore security.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: datastore_security

# Elasticsearch Snapshot Configuration for Automated Backups

# --- docker-compose.yml ---
# Supporting infrastructure for local development/testing

docker-compose.yml: |
  version: '3.8'
  services:
    elasticsearch:
      image: docker.elastic.co/elasticsearch/elasticsearch:8.12.0
      environment:
        - discovery.type=single-node
        - xpack.security.enabled=true
        - xpack.security.transport.ssl.enabled=true
        - ELASTIC_PASSWORD_FILE=/run/secrets/elastic_password
        - path.repo=/usr/share/elasticsearch/backups
        - "ES_JAVA_OPTS=-Xms1g -Xmx1g"
      volumes:
        - es_data:/usr/share/elasticsearch/data
        - es_backups:/usr/share/elasticsearch/backups
      secrets:
        - elastic_password
      ports:
        - "9200:9200"
      healthcheck:
        test: ["CMD-SHELL", "curl -sf --cacert /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/http_ca.crt https://localhost:9200/_cluster/health -u elastic:$$(cat /run/secrets/elastic_password) || exit 1"]
        interval: 30s
        timeout: 10s
        retries: 5

    minio:
      image: minio/minio:latest
      command: server /data --console-address ":9001"
      environment:
        - MINIO_ROOT_USER_FILE=/run/secrets/minio_access_key
        - MINIO_ROOT_PASSWORD_FILE=/run/secrets/minio_secret_key
      volumes:
        - minio_data:/data
      secrets:
        - minio_access_key
        - minio_secret_key
      ports:
        - "9000:9000"
        - "9001:9001"

  volumes:
    es_data:
    es_backups:
    minio_data:

  secrets:
    elastic_password:
      file: ./secrets/elastic_password.txt
    minio_access_key:
      file: ./secrets/minio_access_key.txt
    minio_secret_key:
      file: ./secrets/minio_secret_key.txt

# --- snapshot_config.yml ---
# Main snapshot configuration file

snapshot_config.yml: |
  # Elasticsearch Snapshot Configuration
  # Operations Team - Automated Backup System
  
  elasticsearch:
    # Connection settings - secrets loaded from environment or vault
    hosts:
      - "https://elasticsearch:9200"
    ssl:
      verify_certs: true
      ca_certs: "/etc/elasticsearch/certs/ca.crt"
    # Authentication via environment variables only - never plaintext
    auth:
      username_env: "ES_SNAPSHOT_USERNAME"
      password_env: "ES_SNAPSHOT_PASSWORD"
    # Connection timeouts
    timeout: 30
    max_retries: 3
    retry_on_timeout: true

  repositories:
    # S3-compatible repository configuration
    s3_repository:
      name: "s3-backup-repo"
      type: "s3"
      settings:
        bucket: "elasticsearch-snapshots"
        region: "us-east-1"
        base_path: "snapshots"
        server_side_encryption: true
        storage_class: "standard_ia"
        # Credentials via ES keystore - never in config
        # elasticsearch-keystore add s3.client.default.access_key
        # elasticsearch-keystore add s3.client.default.secret_key
        client: "default"
        compress: true
        chunk_size: "1gb"
        max_restore_bytes_per_sec: "200mb"
        max_snapshot_bytes_per_sec: "100mb"
        readonly: false

    # Shared filesystem repository configuration
    fs_repository:
      name: "fs-backup-repo"
      type: "fs"
      settings:
        location: "/usr/share/elasticsearch/backups"
        compress: true
        chunk_size: "500mb"
        max_restore_bytes_per_sec: "200mb"
        max_snapshot_bytes_per_sec: "100mb"
        readonly: false

  snapshot_policies:
    # Daily snapshot policy
    daily_snapshots:
      name: "daily-snapshot-policy"
      schedule: "0 30 2 * * ?"  # 2:30 AM daily
      repository: "s3-backup-repo"
      config:
        indices:
          - "*"
          - "-.security*"
          - "-.internal*"
        ignore_unavailable: true
        include_global_state: true
        partial: false
        metadata:
          policy: "daily"
          created_by: "ops-automation"
      retention:
        expire_after: "30d"
        min_count: 7
        max_count: 30

    # Weekly snapshot policy
    weekly_snapshots:
      name: "weekly-snapshot-policy"
      schedule: "0 0 3 ? * SUN"  # 3:00 AM every Sunday
      repository: "s3-backup-repo"
      config:
        indices:
          - "*"
        ignore_unavailable: true
        include_global_state: true
        partial: false
        metadata:
          policy: "weekly"
          created_by: "ops-automation"
      retention:
        expire_after: "90d"
        min_count: 4
        max_count: 12

    # Hourly critical indices snapshot
    hourly_critical:
      name: "hourly-critical-policy"
      schedule: "0 0 * * * ?"  # Every hour
      repository: "s3-backup-repo"
      config:
        indices:
          - "critical-*"
          - "production-*"
        ignore_unavailable: true
        include_global_state: false
        partial: false
        metadata:
          policy: "hourly-critical"
          created_by: "ops-automation"
      retention:
        expire_after: "7d"
        min_count: 24
        max_count: 168

  # Allowed index patterns for restore operations (allowlist)
  restore_allowlist:
    - "critical-*"
    - "production-*"
    - "application-*"
    - "logs-*"
    - "metrics-*"

  # Notification settings
  notifications:
    enabled: true
    webhook_url_env: "SNAPSHOT_WEBHOOK_URL"
    notify_on:
      - "snapshot_success"
      - "snapshot_failure"
      - "restore_started"
      - "restore_completed"
      - "restore_failed"
      - "retention_cleanup"

  logging:
    level: "INFO"
    file: "/var/log/elasticsearch-snapshots/snapshot-manager.log"
    max_size: "100MB"
    max_files: 10
    # Never log credentials, tokens, or sensitive data
    redact_patterns:
      - "password"
      - "secret"
      - "token"
      - "key"
      - "authorization"

# --- snapshot_manager.py ---
# Main snapshot management script

snapshot_manager.py: |
  #!/usr/bin/env python3
  """
  Elasticsearch Snapshot Manager
  Secure automated backup creation and restoration for operations team.
  """

  import os
  import re
  import sys
  import json
  import logging
  import hashlib
  import secrets
  import datetime
  from pathlib import Path
  from typing import Optional, Dict, List, Any
  from dataclasses import dataclass, field
  from enum import Enum

  import yaml
  from elasticsearch import Elasticsearch, ElasticsearchException
  from elasticsearch.exceptions import (
      ConnectionError,
      AuthenticationException,
      AuthorizationException,
      NotFoundError,
      TransportError,
  )

  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # Logging with redaction
  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

  class RedactingFilter(logging.Filter):
      """Filter that redacts sensitive information from log records."""

      SENSITIVE_PATTERNS = [
          re.compile(r'(password\s*[=:]\s*)\S+', re.IGNORECASE),
          re.compile(r'(secret\s*[=:]\s*)\S+', re.IGNORECASE),
          re.compile(r'(token\s*[=:]\s*)\S+', re.IGNORECASE),
          re.compile(r'(api[_-]?key\s*[=:]\s*)\S+', re.IGNORECASE),
          re.compile(r'(authorization\s*[=:]\s*)\S+', re.IGNORECASE),
          re.compile(r'(access[_-]?key\s*[=:]\s*)\S+', re.IGNORECASE),
          re.compile(r'(AWS[A-Za-z0-9/+=]{20,})', re.IGNORECASE),
      ]

      def filter(self, record: logging.LogRecord) -> bool:
          if isinstance(record.msg, str):
              for pattern in self.SENSITIVE_PATTERNS:
                  record.msg = pattern.sub(r'\1[REDACTED]', record.msg)
          if record.args:
              new_args = []
              for arg in (record.args if isinstance(record.args, tuple) else (record.args,)):
                  if isinstance(arg, str):
                      for pattern in self.SENSITIVE_PATTERNS:
                          arg = pattern.sub(r'\1[REDACTED]', arg)
                  new_args.append(arg)
              record.args = tuple(new_args)
          return True


  def setup_logging(config: Dict[str, Any]) -> logging.Logger:
      """Configure secure logging with redaction."""
      log_config = config.get('logging', {})
      log_level = getattr(logging, log_config.get('level', 'INFO').upper(), logging.INFO)
      log_file = log_config.get('file', '/var/log/elasticsearch-snapshots/snapshot-manager.log')

      log_dir = Path(log_file).parent
      log_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

      logger = logging.getLogger('snapshot_manager')
      logger.setLevel(log_level)
      logger.addFilter(RedactingFilter())

      from logging.handlers import RotatingFileHandler
      max_bytes = _parse_size(log_config.get('max_size', '100MB'))
      file_handler = RotatingFileHandler(
          log_file,
          maxBytes=max_bytes,
          backupCount=log_config.get('max_files', 10),
      )
      file_handler.setLevel(log_level)
      file_handler.addFilter(RedactingFilter())

      console_handler = logging.StreamHandler(sys.stdout)
      console_handler.setLevel(log_level)
      console_handler.addFilter(RedactingFilter())

      formatter = logging.Formatter(
          '%(asctime)s - %(name)s - %(levelname)s - %(message)s',
          datefmt='%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z',
      )
      file_handler.setFormatter(formatter)
      console_handler.setFormatter(formatter)

      logger.addHandler(file_handler)
      logger.addHandler(console_handler)

      return logger


  def _parse_size(size_str: str) -> int:
      """Parse human-readable size string to bytes."""
      units = {'B': 1, 'KB': 1024, 'MB': 1024**2, 'GB': 1024**3}
      size_str = size_str.upper().strip()
      for unit, multiplier in sorted(units.items(), key=lambda x: -len(x[0])):
          if size_str.endswith(unit):
              try:
                  return int(float(size_str[:-len(unit)].strip()) * multiplier)
              except ValueError:
                  raise ValueError(f"Invalid size string: {size_str}")
      try:
          return int(size_str)
      except ValueError:
          raise ValueError(f"Invalid size string: {size_str}")

  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # Input validation
  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

  class InputValidator:
      """Validates and sanitizes all untrusted input."""

      # Strict allowlist patterns
      REPO_NAME_PATTERN = re.compile(r'^[a-zA-Z0-9][a-zA-Z0-9_\-]{0,99}$')
      SNAPSHOT_NAME_PATTERN = re.compile(r'^[a-zA-Z0-9][a-zA-Z0-9_\-\.]{0,254}$')
      INDEX_PATTERN = re.compile(r'^[a-zA-Z0-9\*][a-zA-Z0-9_\-\.\*]{0,254}$')
      POLICY_NAME_PATTERN = re.compile(r'^[a-zA-Z0-9][a-zA-Z0-9_\-]{0,99}$')

      # Dangerous characters that must never appear in identifiers
      INJECTION_CHARS = re.compile(r'[;&|`$(){}[\]<>!#\'"\\]')

      @classmethod
      def validate_repository_name(cls, name: str) -> str:
          """Validate repository name against strict allowlist."""
          if not isinstance(name, str):
              raise ValueError("Repository name must be a string")
          name = name.strip()
          if cls.INJECTION_CHARS.search(name):
              raise ValueError(f"Repository name contains forbidden characters: {name!r}")
          if not cls.REPO_NAME_PATTERN.match(name):
              raise ValueError(
                  f"Invalid repository name: {name!r}. "
                  "Must start with alphanumeric, contain only [a-zA-Z0-9_-], max 100 chars."
              )
          return name

      @classmethod
      def validate_snapshot_name(cls, name: str) -> str:
          """Validate snapshot name against strict allowlist."""
          if not isinstance(name, str):
              raise ValueError("Snapshot name must be a string")
          name = name.strip().lower()
          if cls.INJECTION_CHARS.search(name):
              raise ValueError(f"Snapshot name contains forbidden characters: {name!r}")
          if not cls.SNAPSHOT_NAME_PATTERN.match(name):
              raise ValueError(
                  f"Invalid snapshot name: {name!r}. "
                  "Must start with alphanumeric, contain only [a-zA-Z0-9_-.], max 255 chars."
              )
          return name

      @classmethod
      def validate_index_pattern(cls, pattern: str) -> str:
          """Validate index pattern against allowlist."""
          if not isinstance(pattern, str):
              raise ValueError("Index pattern must be a string")
          pattern = pattern.strip()
          if cls.INJECTION_CHARS.search(pattern):
              raise ValueError(f"Index pattern contains forbidden characters: {pattern!r}")
          if not cls.INDEX_PATTERN.match(pattern):
              raise ValueError(f"Invalid index pattern: {pattern!r}")
          return pattern

      @classmethod
      def validate_index_against_allowlist(
          cls, index: str, allowlist: List[str]
      ) -> bool:
          """Check if an index matches any pattern in the allowlist."""
          import fnmatch
          validated_index = cls.validate_index_pattern(index)
          for allowed_pattern in allowlist:
              validated_pattern = cls.validate_index_pattern(allowed_pattern)
              if fnmatch.fnmatch(validated_index, validated_pattern):
                  return True
          return False

      @classmethod
      def validate_policy_name(cls, name: str) -> str:
          """Validate policy name against strict allowlist."""
          if not isinstance(name, str):
              raise ValueError("Policy name must be a string")
          name = name.strip()
          if cls.INJECTION_CHARS.search(name):
              raise ValueError(f"Policy name contains forbidden characters: {name!r}")
          if not cls.POLICY_NAME_PATTERN.match(name):
              raise ValueError(f"Invalid policy name: {name!r}")
          return name

      @classmethod
      def validate_path(cls, path_str: str, allowed_base: str) -> str:
          """Validate filesystem path to prevent path traversal."""
          if not isinstance(path_str, str):
              raise ValueError("Path must be a string")
          resolved = Path(path_str).resolve()
          allowed_resolved = Path(allowed_base).resolve()
          if not str(resolved).startswith(str(allowed_resolved)):
              raise ValueError(
                  f"Path traversal detected: {path_str!r} resolves outside {allowed_base!r}"
              )
          return str(resolved)

      @classmethod
      def validate_schedule(cls, schedule: str) -> str:
          """Validate cron schedule expression."""
          if not isinstance(schedule, str):
              raise ValueError("Schedule must be a string")
          schedule = schedule.strip()
          # Basic cron validation: 6-7 space-separated fields
          parts = schedule.split()
          if not (6 <= len(parts) <= 7):
              raise ValueError(f"Invalid cron schedule: {schedule!r}")
          allowed_cron_chars = re.compile(r'^[0-9\*\?\-/,LWABC-Za-z#]+$')
          for part in parts:
              if not allowed_cron_chars.match(part):
                  raise ValueError(f"Invalid cron field: {part!r}")
          return schedule

  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # Secure credential loading
  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

  class SecureCredentialLoader:
      """Load credentials securely from environment variables or secret files."""

      @staticmethod
      def get_credential(env_var: str, secret_file: Optional[str] = None) -> str:
          """
          Load a credential from environment variable or Docker secret file.
          Never returns empty credentials; raises on failure.
          """
          # Try environment variable first
          value = os.environ.get(env_var)
          if value:
              return value

          # Try Docker secrets file
          if secret_file:
              secret_path = Path(secret_file)
              if secret_path.is_file():
                  value = secret_path.read_text().strip()
                  if value:
                      return value

          # Try standard Docker secrets location
          docker_secret_path = Path(f"/run/secrets/{env_var.lower()}")
          if docker_secret_path.is_file():
              value = docker_secret_path.read_text().strip()
              if value:
                  return value

          raise EnvironmentError(
              f"Credential not found. Set environment variable '{env_var}' "
              f"or provide a secret file."
          )

      @staticmethod
      def get_optional_credential(env_var: str) -> Optional[str]:
          """Load an optional credential; returns None if not found."""
          try:
              return SecureCredentialLoader.get_credential(env_var)
          except EnvironmentError:
              return None

  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # Notification helper
  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

  class NotificationManager:
      """Send notifications about snapshot operations."""

      def __init__(self, config: Dict[str, Any], logger: logging.Logger):
          self.config = config.get('notifications', {})
          self.logger = logger
          self.enabled = self.config.get('enabled', False)
          self.notify_on = set(self.config.get('notify_on', []))

      def notify(self, event_type: str, message: str, details: Optional[Dict] = None):
          """Send notification if enabled and event type is configured."""
          if not self.enabled or event_type not in self.notify_on:
              return

          webhook_url = SecureCredentialLoader.get_optional_credential(
              self.config.get('webhook_url_env', 'SNAPSHOT_WEBHOOK_URL')
          )
          if not webhook_url:
              self.logger.warning("Notification webhook URL not configured")
              return

          payload = {
              'event': event_type,
              'message': message,
              'timestamp': datetime.datetime.utcnow().isoformat() + 'Z',
              'details': details or {},
          }

          try:
              import urllib.request
              import ssl

              # Always verify TLS certificates
              ssl_context = ssl.create_default_context()
              req = urllib.request.Request(
                  webhook_url,
                  data=json.dumps(payload).encode('utf-8'),
                  headers={'Content-Type': 'application/json'},
                  method='POST',
              )
              with urllib.request.urlopen(req, context=ssl_context, timeout=10) as resp:
                  if resp.status == 200:
                      self.logger.info(f"Notification sent for event: {event_type}")
                  else:
                      self.logger.warning(
                          f"Notification returned status {resp.status} for event: {event_type}"
                      )
          except Exception as e:
              self.logger.error(f"Failed to send notification for {event_type}: {e}")

  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # Elasticsearch Snapshot Manager
  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

  class SnapshotManager:
      """Manages Elasticsearch snapshot operations securely."""

      def __init__(self, config_path: str):
          self.config = self._load_config(config_path)
          self.logger = setup_logging(self.config)
          self.notifier = NotificationManager(self.config, self.logger)
          self.es = self._create_client()
          self.restore_allowlist = self.config.get('restore_allowlist', [])

      def _load_config(self, config_path: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
          """Load and validate configuration file."""
          path = Path(config_path).resolve()
          if not path.is_file():
              raise FileNotFoundError(f"Configuration file not found: {config_path}")

          with open(path, 'r') as f:
              config = yaml.safe_load(f)

          if not isinstance(config, dict):
              raise ValueError("Configuration must be a YAML mapping")

          return config

      def _create_client(self) -> Elasticsearch:
          """Create Elasticsearch client with secure settings."""
          es_config = self.config.get('elasticsearch', {})
          hosts = es_config.get('hosts', ['https://localhost:9200'])

          # Load credentials securely from environment
          auth_config = es_config.get('auth', {})
          username = SecureCredentialLoader.get_credential(
              auth_config.get('username_env', 'ES_SNAPSHOT_USERNAME')
          )
          password = SecureCredentialLoader.get_credential(
              auth_config.get('password_env', 'ES_SNAPSHOT_PASSWORD')
          )

          ssl_config = es_config.get('ssl', {})
          ca_certs = ssl_config.get('ca_certs')
          verify_certs = ssl_config.get('verify_certs', True)

          if not verify_certs:
              raise ValueError(
                  "SSL certificate verification must be enabled. "
                  "Set ssl.verify_certs to true."
              )

          client_kwargs = {
              'hosts': hosts,
              'basic_auth': (username, password),
              'verify_certs': True,
              'request_timeout': es_config.get('timeout', 30),
              'max_retries': es_config.get('max_retries', 3),
              'retry_on_timeout': es_config.get('retry_on_timeout', True),
          }

          if ca_certs:
              client_kwargs['ca_certs'] = ca_certs

          client = Elasticsearch(**client_kwargs)

          # Verify connectivity
          if not client.ping():
              raise ConnectionError("Cannot connect to Elasticsearch cluster")

          self.logger.info("Successfully connected to Elasticsearch cluster")
          return client

      # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
      # Repository management
      # -----------------------------------------------------------------------

      def create_repository(self, repo_key: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
          """Create a snapshot repository from configuration."""
          repos_config = self.config.get('repositories', {})
          if repo_key not in repos_config:
              raise ValueError(f"Repository configuration '{repo_key}' not found")

          repo_config = repos_config[repo_key]
          repo_name = InputValidator.validate_repository_name(repo_config['name'])
          repo_type = repo_config['type']

          if repo_type not in ('s3', 'fs', 'gcs', 'azure'):
              raise ValueError(f"Unsupported repository type: {repo_type}")

          settings = dict(repo_config.get('settings', {}))

          # Validate filesystem path if fs type
          if repo_type == 'fs' and 'location' in settings:
              settings['location'] = InputValidator.validate_path(
                  settings['location'],
                  '/usr/share/elasticsearch/backups',
              )

          try:
              response = self.es.snapshot.create_repository(
                  name=repo_name,
                  body={
                      'type': repo_type,
                      'settings': settings,
                  },
              )
              self.logger.info(f"Repository '{repo_name}' created successfully")
              return response
          except ElasticsearchException as e:
              self.logger.error(f"Failed to create repository '{repo_name}': {e}")
              raise

      def verify_repository(self, repo_name: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
          """Verify a snapshot repository is functional."""
          repo_name = InputValidator.validate_repository_name(repo_name)
          try:
              response = self.es.snapshot.verify_repository(name=repo_name)
              self.logger.info(f"Repository '{repo_name}' verified successfully")
              return response
          except ElasticsearchException as e:
              self.logger.error(f"Repository verification failed for '{repo_name}': {e}")
              raise

      def get_repository(self, repo_name: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
          """Get repository information."""
          repo_name = InputValidator.validate_repository_name(repo_name)
          try:
              return self.es.snapshot.get_repository(name=repo_name)
          except NotFoundError:
              self.logger.warning(f"Repository '{repo_name}' not found")
              raise
          except ElasticsearchException as e:
              self.logger.error(f"Failed to get repository '{repo_name}': {e}")
              raise

      def list_repositories(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
          """List all snapshot repositories."""
          try:
              return self.es.snapshot.get_repository(name='*')
          except ElasticsearchException as e:
              self.logger.error(f"Failed to list repositories: {e}")
              raise

      def delete_repository(self, repo_name: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
          """Delete a snapshot repository."""
          repo_name = InputValidator.validate_repository_name(repo_name)
          try:
              response = self.es.snapshot.delete_repository(name=repo_name)
              self.logger.info(f"Repository '{repo_name}' deleted")
              return response
          except ElasticsearchException as e:
              self.logger.error(f"Failed to delete repository '{repo_name}': {e}")
              raise

      # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
      # Snapshot creation
      # -----------------------------------------------------------------------

      def create_snapshot(
          self,
          repo_name: str,
          snapshot_name: Optional[str] = None,
          indices: Optional[List[str]] = None,
          include_global_state: bool = True,
          ignore_unavailable: bool = True,
          partial: bool = False,
          metadata: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None,
          wait_for_completion: bool = False,
      ) -> Dict[str, Any]:
          """Create a snapshot with validated parameters."""
          repo_name = InputValidator.validate_repository_name(repo_name)

          if snapshot_name is None:
              timestamp = datetime.datetime.utcnow().strftime('%Y%m%d-%H%M%S')
              random_suffix = secrets.token_hex(4)
              snapshot_name = f"snapshot-{timestamp}-{random_suffix}"

          snapshot_name = InputValidator.validate_snapshot_name(snapshot_name)

          # Validate indices
          validated_indices = []
          if indices:
              for idx in indices:
                  validated_indices.append(InputValidator.validate_index_pattern(idx))

          body = {
              'ignore_unavailable': bool(ignore_unavailable),
              'include_global_state': bool(include_global_state),
              'partial': bool(partial),
              'metadata': {
                  'created_by': 'snapshot-manager',
                  'created_at': datetime.datetime.utcnow().isoformat() + 'Z',
                  **(metadata or {}),
              },
          }

          if validated_indices:
              body['indices'] = ','.join(validated_indices)

          try:
              self.logger.info(
                  f"Creating snapshot '{snapshot_name}' in repository '{repo_name}'"
              )
              response = self.es.snapshot.create(
                  repository=repo_name,
                  snapshot=snapshot_name,
                  body=body,
                  wait_for_completion=wait_for_completion,
              )
              self.logger.info(
                  f"Snapshot '{snapshot_name}' creation initiated in '{repo_name}'"
              )
              self.notifier.notify(
                  'snapshot_success',
                  f"Snapshot '{snapshot_name}' created in '{repo_name}'",
                  {'repository': repo_name, 'snapshot': snapshot_name},
              )
              return response
          except ElasticsearchException as e:
              self.logger.error(
                  f"Failed to create snapshot '{snapshot_name}' in '{repo_name}': {e}"
              )
              self.notifier.notify(
                  'snapshot_failure',
                  f"Snapshot creation failed: {e}",
                  {'repository': repo_name, 'snapshot': snapshot_name},
              )
              raise

      def get_snapshot(self, repo_name: str, snapshot_name: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
          """Get snapshot information."""
          repo_name = InputValidator.validate_repository_name(repo_name)
          snapshot_name = InputValidator.validate_snapshot_name(snapshot_name)
          try:
              return self.es.snapshot.get(repository=repo_name, snapshot=snapshot_name)
          except ElasticsearchException as e:
              self.logger.error(
                  f"Failed to get snapshot '{snapshot_name}' from '{repo_name}': {e}"
              )
              raise

      def list_snapshots(self, repo_name: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
          """List all snapshots in a repository."""
          repo_name = InputValidator.validate_repository_name(repo_name)
          try:
              return self.es.snapshot.get(repository=repo_name, snapshot='*')
          except ElasticsearchException as e:
              self.logger.error(f"Failed to list snapshots in '{repo_name}': {e}")
              raise

      def get_snapshot_status(
          self, repo_name: str, snapshot_name: str
      ) -> Dict[str, Any]:
          """Get detailed snapshot status."""
          repo_name = InputValidator.validate_repository_name(repo_name)
          snapshot_name = InputValidator.validate_snapshot_name(snapshot_name)
          try:
              return self.es.snapshot.status(
                  repository=repo_name, snapshot=snapshot_name
              )
          except ElasticsearchException as e:
              self.logger.error(
                  f"Failed to get status for '{snapshot_name}' in '{repo_name}': {e}"
              )
              raise

      def delete_snapshot(self, repo_name: str, snapshot_name: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
          """Delete a snapshot."""
          repo_name = InputValidator.validate_repository_name(repo_name)
          snapshot_name = InputValidator.validate_snapshot_name(snapshot_name)
          try:
              response = self.es.snapshot.delete(
                  repository=repo_name, snapshot=snapshot_name
              )
              self.logger.info(
                  f"Snapshot '{snapshot_name}' deleted from '{repo_name}'"
              )
              return response
          except ElasticsearchException as e:
              self.logger.error(
                  f"Failed to delete snapshot '{snapshot_name}' from '{repo_name}': {e}"
              )
              raise

      # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
      # Snapshot restoration
      # -----------------------------------------------------------------------

      def restore_snapshot(
          self,
          repo_name: str,
          snapshot_name: str,
          indices: Optional[List[str]] = None,
          rename_pattern: Optional[str] = None,
          rename_replacement: Optional[str] = None,
          include_global_state: bool = False,
          ignore_unavailable: bool = True,
          partial: bool = False,
          index_settings: Optional[Dict] = None,
          ignore_index_settings: Optional[List[str]] = None,
          wait_for_completion: bool = False,
      ) -> Dict[str, Any]:
          """
          Restore a snapshot with strict validation and allowlist enforcement.
          """
          repo_name = InputValidator.validate_repository_name(repo_name)
          snapshot_name = InputValidator.validate_snapshot_name(snapshot_name)

          # Validate and enforce index allowlist
          validated_indices = []
          if indices:
              for idx in indices:
                  validated_idx = InputValidator.validate_index_pattern(idx)
                  if self.restore_allowlist:
                      if not InputValidator.validate_index_against_allowlist(
                          validated_idx, self.restore_allowlist
                      ):
                          raise ValueError(
                              f"Index '{validated_idx}' is not in the restore allowlist. "
                              f"Allowed patterns: {self.restore_allowlist}"
                          )
                  validated_indices.append(validated_idx)

          # Validate rename pattern to prevent injection
          if rename_pattern:
              if not re.match(r'^[a-zA-Z0-9_\-\.\(\)\[\]\\]+$', rename_pattern):
                  raise ValueError(f"Invalid rename pattern: {rename_pattern!r}")
          if rename_replacement:
              if not re.match(r'^[a-zA-Z0-9_\-\.\$]+$', rename_replacement):
                  raise ValueError(f"Invalid rename replacement: {rename_replacement!r}")

          # Validate index_settings keys against allowlist
          ALLOWED_INDEX_SETTINGS = {
              'index.number_of_replicas',
              'index.refresh_interval',
              'index.codec',
              'index.routing.allocation.include._tier_preference',
              'index.routing.allocation.require._name',
              'index.routing.allocation.exclude._name',
          }
          if index_settings:
              for key in index_settings:
                  if key not in ALLOWED_INDEX_SETTINGS:
                      raise ValueError(
                          f"Index setting '{key}' is not in the allowed settings list"
                      )

          body: Dict[str, Any] = {
              'ignore_unavailable': bool(ignore_unavailable),
              'include_global_state': bool(include_global_state),
              'partial': bool(partial),
          }

          if validated_indices:
              body['indices'] = ','.join(validated_indices)
          if rename_pattern:
              body['rename_pattern'] = rename_pattern
          if rename_replacement:
              body['rename_replacement'] = rename_replacement
          if index_settings:
              body['index_settings'] = index_settings
          if ignore_index_settings:
              body['ignore_index_settings'] = ignore_index_settings

          try:
              self.logger.info(
                  f"Restoring snapshot '{snapshot_name}' from '{repo_name}'"
              )
              self.notifier.notify(
                  'restore_started',
                  f"Restore started: '{snapshot_name}' from '{repo_name}'",
                  {
                      'repository': repo_name,
                      'snapshot': snapshot_name,
                      'indices': validated_indices,
                  },
              )

              response = self.es.snapshot.restore(
                  repository=repo_name,
                  snapshot=snapshot_name,
                  body=body,
                  wait_for_completion=wait_for_completion,
              )

              self.logger.info(
                  f"Snapshot '{snapshot_name}' restore initiated from '{repo_name}'"
              )
              self.notifier.notify(
                  'restore_completed',
                  f"Restore completed: '{snapshot_name}' from '{repo_name}'",
                  {'repository': repo_name, 'snapshot': snapshot_name},
              )
              return response
          except ElasticsearchException as e:
              self.logger.error(
                  f"Failed to restore snapshot '{snapshot_name}' from '{repo_name}': {e}"
              )
              self.notifier.notify(
                  'restore_failed',
                  f"Restore failed: {e}",
                  {'repository': repo_name, 'snapshot': snapshot_name},
              )
              raise

      # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
      # SLM (Snapshot Lifecycle Management) policies
      # -----------------------------------------------------------------------

      def create_slm_policy(self, policy_key: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
          """Create an SLM policy from configuration."""
          policies_config = self.config.get('snapshot_policies', {})
          if policy_key not in policies_config:
              raise ValueError(f"Policy configuration '{policy_key}' not found")

          policy_config = policies_config[policy_key]
          policy_name = InputValidator.validate_policy_name(policy_config['name'])
          repository = InputValidator.validate_repository_name(policy_config['repository'])
          schedule = InputValidator.validate_schedule(policy_config['schedule'])

          snap_config = policy_config.get('config', {})
          validated_indices = []
          for idx in snap_config.get('indices', ['*']):
              validated_indices.append(InputValidator.validate_index_pattern(idx))

          retention = policy_config.get('retention', {})

          body = {
              'schedule': schedule,
              'name': f"<{policy_name}-{{now/d}}-{{now/H}}>",
              'repository': repository,
              'config': {
                  'indices': validated_indices,
                  'ignore_unavailable': snap_config.get('ignore_unavailable', True),
                  'include_global_state': snap_config.get('include_global_state', True),
                  'partial': snap_config.get('partial', False),
                  'metadata': snap_config.get('metadata', {}),
              },
              'retention': {},
          }

          if 'expire_after' in retention:
              body['retention']['expire_after'] = retention['expire_after']
          if 'min_count' in retention:
              body['retention']['min_count'] = int(retention['min_count'])
          if 'max_count' in retention:
              body['retention']['max_count'] = int(retention['max_count'])

          try:
              response = self.es.slm.put_lifecycle(policy_id=policy_name, body=body)
              self.logger.info(f"SLM policy '{policy_name}' created successfully")
              return response
          except ElasticsearchException as e:
              self.logger.error(f"Failed to create SLM policy '{policy_name}': {e}")
              raise

      def execute_slm_policy(self, policy_name: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
          """Manually execute an SLM policy."""
          policy_name = InputValidator.validate_policy_name(policy_name)
          try:
              response = self.es.slm.execute_lifecycle(policy_id=policy_name)
              self.logger.info(f"SLM policy '{policy_name}' executed")
              return response
          except ElasticsearchException as e:
              self.logger.error(f"Failed to execute SLM policy '{policy_name}': {e}")
              raise

      def get_slm_policy(self, policy_name: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
          """Get SLM policy details."""
          policy_name = InputValidator.validate_policy_name(policy_name)
          try:
              return self.es.slm.get_lifecycle(policy_id=policy_name)
          except ElasticsearchException as e:
              self.logger.error(f"Failed to get SLM policy '{policy_name}': {e}")
              raise

      def list_slm_policies(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
          """List all SLM policies."""
          try:
              return self.es.slm.get_lifecycle()
          except ElasticsearchException as e:
              self.logger.error(f"Failed to list SLM policies: {e}")
              raise

      def delete_slm_policy(self, policy_name: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
          """Delete an SLM policy."""
          policy_name = InputValidator.validate_policy_name(policy_name)
          try:
              response = self.es.slm.delete_lifecycle(policy_id=policy_name)
              self.logger.info(f"SLM policy '{policy_name}' deleted")
              return response
          except ElasticsearchException as e:
              self.logger.error(f"Failed to delete SLM policy '{policy_name}': {e}")
              raise

      def execute_retention(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
          """Manually trigger SLM retention cleanup."""
          try:
              response = self.es.slm.execute_retention()
              self.logger.info("SLM retention cleanup executed")
              self.notifier.notify(
                  'retention_cleanup',
                  'SLM retention cleanup executed',
              )
              return response
          except ElasticsearchException as e:
              self.logger.error(f"Failed to execute retention: {e}")
              raise

      def get_slm_stats(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
          """Get SLM statistics."""
          try:
              return self.es.slm.get_stats()
          except ElasticsearchException as e:
              self.logger.error(f"Failed to get SLM stats: {e}")
              raise

      # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
      # Setup all configured resources
      # -----------------------------------------------------------------------

      def setup_all(self):
          """Set up all configured repositories and policies."""
          self.logger.info("Starting full snapshot infrastructure setup")

          # Create repositories
          for repo_key in self.config.get('repositories', {}):
              try:
                  self.create_repository(repo_key)
                  repo_name = self.config['repositories'][repo_key]['name']
                  self.verify_repository(repo_name)
              except Exception as e:
                  self.logger.error(f"Failed to set up repository '{repo_key}': {e}")

          # Create SLM policies
          for policy_key in self.config.get('snapshot_policies', {}):
              try:
                  self.create_slm_policy(policy_key)
              except Exception as e:
                  self.logger.error(f"Failed to set up policy '{policy_key}': {e}")

          self.logger.info("Snapshot infrastructure setup complete")

      # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
      # Health check
      # -----------------------------------------------------------------------

      def health_check(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
          """Perform a comprehensive health check of snapshot infrastructure."""
          results: Dict[str, Any] = {
              'timestamp': datetime.datetime.utcnow().isoformat() + 'Z',
              'cluster_health': None,
              'repositories': {},
              'policies': {},
              'overall_status': 'healthy',
          }

          # Cluster health
          try:
              results['cluster_health'] = self.es.cluster.health()
          except ElasticsearchException as e:
              results['cluster_health'] = {'error': str(e)}
              results['overall_status'] = 'unhealthy'

          # Repository verification
          for repo_key, repo_config in self.config.get('repositories', {}).items():
              repo_name = repo_config['name']
              try:
                  verification = self.verify_repository(repo_name)
                  results['repositories'][repo_name] = {
                      'status': 'verified',
                      'details': verification,
                  }
              except NotFoundError:
                  results['repositories'][repo_name] = {'status': 'not_found'}
                  results['overall_status'] = 'degraded'
              except ElasticsearchException as e:
                  results['repositories'][repo_name] = {
                      'status': 'error',
                      'error': str(e),
                  }
                  results['overall_status'] = 'unhealthy'

          # SLM policy status
          try:
              policies = self.list_slm_policies()
              for policy_name, policy_data in policies.items():
                  last_success = policy_data.get('last_success', {})
                  last_failure = policy_data.get('last_failure', {})
                  results['policies'][policy_name] = {
                      'last_success': last_success.get('time'),
                      'last_failure': last_failure.get('time'),
                      'next_execution': policy_data.get('next_execution'),
                  }
                  if last_failure.get('time') and (
                      not last_success.get('time')
                      or last_failure['time'] > last_success.get('time', '')
                  ):
                      results['overall_status'] = 'degraded'
          except ElasticsearchException as e:
              results['policies'] = {'error': str(e)}

          self.logger.info(f"Health check completed: {results['overall_status']}")
          return results

  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # CLI entry point
  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

  def main():
      """CLI entry point for snapshot manager."""
      import argparse

      parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
          description='Elasticsearch Snapshot Manager',
          formatter_class=argparse.RawDescriptionHelpFormatter,
      )
      parser.add_argument(
          '-c', '--config',
          default='snapshot_config.yml',
          help='Path to configuration file',
      )

      subparsers = parser.add_subparsers(dest='command', help='Available commands')

      # Setup
      subparsers.add_parser('setup', help='Set up all repositories and policies')

      # Health check
      subparsers.add_parser('health', help='Run health check')

      # Repository commands
      repo_parser = subparsers.add_parser('repo', help='Repository operations')
      repo_sub = repo_parser.add_subparsers(dest='repo_command')
      repo_create = repo_sub.add_parser('create', help='Create repository')
      repo_create.add_argument('repo_key', help='Repository config key')
      repo_verify = repo_sub.add_parser('verify', help='Verify repository')
      repo_verify.add_argument('repo_name', help='Repository name')
      repo_sub.add_parser('list', help='List repositories')
      repo_delete = repo_sub.add_parser('delete', help='Delete repository')
      repo_delete.add_argument('repo_name', help='Repository name')

      # Snapshot commands
      snap_parser = subparsers.add_parser('snapshot', help='Snapshot operations')
      snap_sub = snap_parser.add_subparsers(dest='snap_command')

      snap_create = snap_sub.add_parser('create', help='Create snapshot')
      snap_create.add_argument('repo_name', help='Repository name')
      snap_create.add_argument('--name', help='Snapshot name (auto-generated if omitted)')
      snap_create.add_argument('--indices', nargs='+', help='Index patterns')
      snap_create.add_argument('--wait', action='store_true', help='Wait for completion')

      snap_list = snap_sub.add_parser('list', help='List snapshots')
      snap_list.add_argument('repo_name', help='Repository name')

      snap_status = snap_sub.add_parser('status', help='Get snapshot status')
      snap_status.add_argument('repo_name', help='Repository name')
      snap_status.add_argument('snapshot_name', help='Snapshot name')

      snap_delete = snap_sub.add_parser('delete', help='Delete snapshot')
      snap_delete.add_argument('repo_name', help='Repository name')
      snap_delete.add_argument('snapshot_name', help='Snapshot name')

      # Restore commands
      restore_parser = subparsers.add_parser('restore', help='Restore operations')
      restore_parser.add_argument('repo_name', help='Repository name')
      restore_parser.add_argument('snapshot_name', help='Snapshot name')
      restore_parser.add_argument('--indices', nargs='+', help='Index patterns to restore')
      restore_parser.add_argument('--rename-pattern', help='Rename pattern')
      restore_parser.add_argument('--rename-replacement', help='Rename replacement')
      restore_parser.add_argument('--wait', action='store_true', help='Wait for completion')

      # Policy commands
      policy_parser = subparsers.add_parser('policy', help='SLM policy operations')
      policy_sub = policy_parser.add_subparsers(dest='policy_command')

      policy_create = policy_sub.add_parser('create', help='Create SLM policy')
      policy_create.add_argument('policy_key', help='Policy config key')

      policy_exec = policy_sub.add_parser('execute', help='Execute SLM policy')
      policy_exec.add_argument('policy_name', help='Policy name')

      policy_sub.add_parser('list', help='List SLM policies')

      policy_delete = policy_sub.add_parser('delete', help='Delete SLM policy')
      policy_delete.add_argument('policy_name', help='Policy name')

      policy_sub.add_parser('retention', help='Execute retention cleanup')
      policy_sub.add_parser('stats', help='Get SLM statistics')

      args = parser.parse_args()

      if not args.command:
          parser.print_help()
          sys.exit(1)

      try:
          manager = SnapshotManager(args.config)
      except Exception as e:
          print(f"Error initializing snapshot manager: {e}", file=sys.stderr)
          sys.exit(1)

      try:
          if args.command == 'setup':
              manager.setup_all()

          elif args.command == 'health':
              result = manager.health_check()
              print(json.dumps(result, indent=2, default=str))

          elif args.command == 'repo':
              if args.repo_command == 'create':
                  result = manager.create_repository(args.repo_key)
              elif args.repo_command == 'verify':
                  result = manager.verify_repository(args.repo_name)
              elif args.repo_command == 'list':
                  result = manager.list_repositories()
              elif args.repo_command == 'delete':
                  result = manager.delete_repository(args.repo_name)
              else:
                  repo_parser.print_help()
                  sys.exit(1)
              print(json.dumps(result, indent=2, default=str))

          elif args.command == 'snapshot':
              if args.snap_command == 'create':
                  result = manager.create_snapshot(
                      repo_name=args.repo_name,
                      snapshot_name=args.name,
                      indices=args.indices,
                      wait_for_completion=args.wait,
                  )
              elif args.snap_command == 'list':
                  result = manager.list_snapshots(args.repo_name)
              elif args.snap_command == 'status':
                  result = manager.get_snapshot_status(args.repo_name, args.snapshot_name)
              elif args.snap_command == 'delete':
                  result = manager.delete_snapshot(args.repo_name, args.snapshot_name)
              else:
                  snap_parser.print_help()
                  sys.exit(1)
              print(json.dumps(result, indent=2, default=str))

          elif args.command == 'restore':
              result = manager.restore_snapshot(
                  repo_name=args.repo_name,
                  snapshot_name=args.snapshot_name,
                  indices=args.indices,
                  rename_pattern=args.rename_pattern,
                  rename_replacement=args.rename_replacement,
                  wait_for_completion=args.wait,
              )
              print(json.dumps(result, indent=2, default=str))

          elif args.command == 'policy':
              if args.policy_command == 'create':
                  result = manager.create_slm_policy(args.policy_key)
              elif args.policy_command == 'execute':
                  result = manager.execute_slm_policy(args.policy_name)
              elif args.policy_command == 'list':
                  result = manager.list_slm_policies()
              elif args.policy_command == 'delete':
                  result = manager.delete_slm_policy(args.policy_name)
              elif args.policy_command == 'retention':
                  result = manager.execute_retention()
              elif args.policy_command == 'stats':
                  result = manager.get_slm_stats()
              else:
                  policy_parser.print_help()
                  sys.exit(1)
              print(json.dumps(result, indent=2, default=str))

      except (ValueError, EnvironmentError) as e:
          print(f"Validation error: {e}", file=sys.stderr)
          sys.exit(1)
      except AuthenticationException:
          print("Authentication failed. Check credentials.", file=sys.stderr)
          sys.exit(2)
      except AuthorizationException:
          print("Authorization failed. Insufficient permissions.", file=sys.stderr)
          sys.exit(3)
      except ConnectionError as e:
          print(f"Connection error: {e}", file=sys.stderr)
          sys.exit(4)
      except ElasticsearchException as e:
          print(f"Elasticsearch error: {e}", file=sys.stderr)
          sys.exit(5)


  if __name__ == '__main__':
      main()

# --- es_keystore_setup.sh ---
# Script to securely configure Elasticsearch keystore for S3 credentials

es_keystore_setup.sh: |
  #!/usr/bin/env bash
  set -euo pipefail

  # Elasticsearch Keystore Setup for S3 Repository Credentials
  # This script reads secrets from files or prompts interactively.
  # It NEVER accepts credentials as command-line arguments (visible in ps).

  ES_HOME="${ES_HOME:-/usr/share/elasticsearch}"
  KEYSTORE_CMD="${ES_HOME}/bin/elasticsearch-keystore"

  echo "=== Elasticsearch Keystore Setup for S3 Snapshot Repository ==="

  # Ensure keystore exists
  if [ ! -f "${ES_HOME}/config/elasticsearch.keystore" ]; then
      echo "Creating new keystore..."
      "${KEYSTORE_CMD}" create
  fi

  # Function to add secret from file or stdin
  add_secret() {
      local key="$1"
      local secret_file="$2"

      if [ -n "${secret_file}" ] && [ -f "${secret_file}" ]; then
          echo "Adding ${key} from secret file..."
          "${KEYSTORE_CMD}" add "${key}" --stdin < "${secret_file}"
      else
          echo "Enter value for ${key}:"
          "${KEYSTORE_CMD}" add "${key}" --stdin
      fi
  }

  # S3 access key
  S3_ACCESS_KEY_FILE="${S3_ACCESS_KEY_FILE:-/run/secrets/s3_access_key}"
  add_secret "s3.client.default.access_key" "${S3_ACCESS_KEY_FILE}"

  # S3 secret key
  S3_SECRET_KEY_FILE="${S3_SECRET_KEY_FILE:-/run/secrets/s3_secret_key}"
  add_secret "s3.client.default.secret_key" "${S3_SECRET_KEY_FILE}"

  echo "=== Keystore setup complete ==="
  echo "Restart Elasticsearch to apply changes:"
  echo "  systemctl restart elasticsearch"

# --- snapshot_role.yml ---
# Minimal privilege role for snapshot operations

snapshot_role.yml: |
  # Elasticsearch role for snapshot management - least privilege
  # Apply via: PUT _security/role/snapshot_manager
  
  snapshot_manager_role:
    cluster:
      - "manage_slm"
      - "cluster:admin/snapshot/create"
      - "cluster:admin/snapshot/delete"
      - "cluster:admin/snapshot/get"
      - "cluster:admin/snapshot/restore"
      - "cluster:admin/snapshot/status"
      - "cluster:admin/repository/put"
      - "cluster:admin/repository/get"
      - "cluster:admin/repository/delete"
      - "cluster:admin/repository/verify"
      - "monitor"
    indices:
      - names:
          - "*"
        privileges:
          - "monitor"
          - "read"
        allow_restricted_indices: false

  # Read-only role for monitoring snapshots
  snapshot_monitor_role:
    cluster:
      - "monitor_snapshot"
      - "cluster:admin/snapshot/get"
      - "cluster:admin/snapshot/status"
      - "cluster:admin/repository/get"
      - "monitor"
    indices:
      - names:
          - "*"
        privileges:
          - "monitor"
        allow_restricted_indices: false

# --- setup_roles.yml ---
# Ansible-style playbook for setting up roles and users

setup_roles.yml: |
  # Setup script for Elasticsearch security roles
  # Run with appropriate admin credentials
  
  # Create snapshot manager role
  # PUT _security/role/snapshot_manager
  snapshot_manager_role:
    cluster:
      - manage_slm
      - create_snapshot
      - monitor
    indices:
      - names: ["*"]
        privileges: ["monitor", "read"]
        allow_restricted_indices: false
  
  # Create snapshot manager user
  # PUT _security/user/snapshot_automation
  snapshot_automation_user:
    roles:
      - snapshot_manager
    full_name: "Snapshot Automation Service"
    email: "ops-snapshots@example.com"
    # Password must be set via API with secure transport
    # Never store in configuration files
    metadata:
      service_account: true
      managed_by: "ops-automation"

# --- crontab_entry ---
# System crontab for running snapshot health checks

crontab_entry: |
  # Elasticsearch Snapshot Health Check - runs every 15 minutes
  */15 * * * * /opt/snapshot-manager/venv/bin/python /opt/snapshot-manager/snapshot_manager.py -c /etc/elasticsearch-snapshots/snapshot_config.yml health >> /var/log/elasticsearch-snapshots/health-check.log 2>&1

# --- requirements.txt ---
requirements.txt: |
  elasticsearch>=8.12.0,<9.0.0
  pyyaml>=6.0.1,<7.0.0
  urllib3>=2.0.0,<3.0.0
  certifi>=2024.2.2

# --- Makefile ---
Makefile: |
  .PHONY: setup install test health snapshot-create snapshot-list restore

  PYTHON := python3
  VENV := .venv
  CONFIG := snapshot_config.yml

  install:
  	$(PYTHON) -m venv $(VENV)
  	$(VENV)/bin/pip install -r requirements.txt

  setup: install
  	$(VENV)/bin/python snapshot_manager.py -c $(CONFIG) setup

  health:
  	$(VENV)/bin/python snapshot_manager.py -c $(CONFIG) health

  repo-list:
  	$(VENV)/bin/python snapshot_manager.py -c $(CONFIG) repo list

  snapshot-create:
  	$(VENV)/bin/python snapshot_manager.py -c $(CONFIG) snapshot create $(REPO) --wait

  snapshot-list:
  	$(VENV)/bin/python snapshot_manager.py -c $(CONFIG) snapshot list $(REPO)

  policy-list:
  	$(VENV)/bin/python snapshot_manager.py -c $(CONFIG) policy list

  policy-stats:
  	$(VENV)/bin/python snapshot_manager.py -c $(CONFIG) policy stats
